message:
feat(runtime-compat): integrate sleep/timeout with Cx budgets and virtual time

Core runtime migration: sleep() and timeout() now respect Cx budget
deadlines and work with LabRuntime virtual time.

runtime_compat.rs (+210 lines):
- sleep() resolves active Cx and delegates to new sleep_with_cx() which
  calls budget_sleep instead of plain sleep — sleeps now short-circuit
  when the budget deadline expires
- New timeout_with_cx() calls budget_timeout — Cx budget deadlines
  become the "tighter" bound over explicit timeouts
- New cx_timer_now() helper for consistent virtual time source
- 4 new tests: budget exhaustion short-circuits sleep, LabRuntime
  virtual time auto-advance, budget wins over explicit timeout
- 2 proptest properties: Budget::meet() selects earliest deadline,
  LabRuntime timers fire in deadline order

cx.rs:
- spawn_with_timeout now uses timeout_with_cx under asupersync-runtime,
  so a 20ms budget deadline wins over a 5s explicit timeout
- New test: spawn_with_timeout_uses_tighter_cx_budget

runtime.rs:
- Feature-gate mut on stream_exit_rx for asupersync channel API
  compatibility (recv takes &self, not &mut self)

orphan_reaper.rs:
- Migrate tokio::time::sleep to runtime_compat::sleep

mux_pool.rs:
- Fix type inference: Err::<(), _>(...) explicit annotation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
